headline: Colgate-Palmolive Announces Quarterly Dividend Increase and Elects Christopher Boerner, Ph.D. to Board of Directors
summary: NEW YORK, March 12, 2026--The Board of Directors of Colgate-Palmolive Company (NYSE:CL) today announced an increase in the quarterly common stock cash dividend to $0.53 up from $0.52 per share. The increase will be effective in the second quarter, 2026. The Board declared that the second quarter dividend is to be paid on May 15, 2026, to shareholders of record on April 20, 2026. On an annualized basis, the new dividend rate is $2.12 versus $2.08 per share previously. The Company has paid uninter

headline: Trump urges Powell to cut rates immediately as bond traders scale back Fed easing
summary: Investing.com -- Bond traders have scaled back expectations for Federal Reserve interest rate cuts this year as rising oil prices fuel inflation concerns, while President Donald Trump publicly called for immediate rate reductions.

headline: BoC expected to hold rates amid geopolitical volatility - BofA
summary: Investing.com -- The Bank of Canada is projected to maintain its policy rate at 2.25% through the duration of 2026. According to a report from BofA Securities economist Carlos Capistran, persistent geopolitical tensions and trade uncertainties are expected to anchor the central bank’s current position. headline: Fertilizer stocks surge as Middle East conflict disrupts global supply
summary: The urgency stems from timing that industry analysts describe as catastrophic. Around one-third of global fertilizer supply passes through the Strait of Hormuz, which Iran has threatened to shut to shipping amid the ongoing U.S.-Israeli military campaign. Product shipped through the Middle East during mid-March is set to arrive in North America for April spring planting application and is now at risk of being delayed, according to Farm Progress.

headline: Cruise stocks slide as oil surges to $100 on Iran attacks
summary: Investing.com -- Carnival Corporation (NYSE:CCL), Royal Caribbean Cruises Ltd (F:RCL), Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd (NYSE:NCLH), and Viking Holdings Ltd (NYSE:VIK) all declined in pre-open trading Thursday as crude oil prices surged near $100 per barrel following fresh Iranian attacks on vessels near the Strait of Hormuz, raising concerns about escalating fuel costs across an industry where energy typically accounts for 10%-15% of revenue.

headline: Airline stocks slide as oil surges past $100 on Iran tanker attacks
summary: Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) led declines, dropping 2.7% to $40.73 after ending its fuel hedging program in 2025, leaving it fully exposed to spot market pricing. Delta Air Lines Inc (NYSE:DAL) fell 2% to $57.97, while United Airlines (NASDAQ:UAL) declined 2.2% to $88.73. American Airlines Group (BMV:AAL) fell 2% to $10.82. European airline stocks suffered steeper losses, falling between 2.4% and 5.8% Thursday morning, with Lufthansa, Air France-KLM, easyJet, IAG, Wizz Air, Norwegian Air, and R

headline: Goldman models Brent at $93 in 60-day Hormuz disruption scenario
summary: Investing.com -- Goldman Sachs has raised its oil price forecasts as it assumes a longer and deeper disruption to flows through the Strait of Hormuz, warning that risks remain “skewed to the upside” if outages persist.

headline: How The Colgate Palmolive (CL) Narrative Is Shifting With 2030 Plan And 2026 Outlook
summary: Colgate Palmolive’s modeled fair value was nudged from about US$96.68 to roughly US$97.21, a shift of less than 1% that still catches the eye when you track target moves closely. That tweak sits alongside a flurry of Street research, with some analysts pushing targets into the US$93 to US$105 range, while others trim back toward levels like US$86 as they weigh the company’s 2030 plan against sector headwinds. As you read on, you will see how to follow this evolving narrative and what these...
